Home / SaaS Pricing Planner

SaaS Pricing Planner

Compare flat, tiered, usage-based, and freemium pricing models. Get personalized recommendations based on your product type, audience, and competitor prices.

Scenarios:

How to Use This Tool

1
Select your product type.
2
Select your target customer segment.
3
Enter the average competitor price (or skip if unknown).
4
Review the best-fit pricing model recommendation.
5
Compare all 4 models side by side.
6
tools.solopreneur-saas-pricing-planner.how_to_use.5

Frequently Asked Questions

Which pricing model is best for a new SaaS?

Start with flat-rate or two tiers. Validate willingness to pay first, then expand to three tiers once you understand your power users.

How much should I charge compared to competitors?

If you offer more value, charge 20-50% more. If you want fast market entry, charge 20% less. Never compete on price alone — compete on differentiated value.

What is anchor pricing?

Present a high-priced tier to make your mid-tier look like a bargain. The middle tier becomes the obvious choice for most buyers.

Should I show pricing on my landing page?

Yes. Transparent pricing builds trust. If you must hide it (enterprise deals), at least show a starting price or range.

When should I raise prices?

Raise prices when your product has significantly more value than at launch, or when demand outpaces your capacity. Grandfather existing customers to avoid churn.

Results

Example

🎯 Pricing Recommendations for SaaS

👤 Target: b2b 💰 Competitor Price: $29 ⭐ BEST FIT: Tiered Pricing Products with power users who will pay more for advanced features. Suggested Price Points: • Starter: $15/mo • Pro (recommended): $29/mo • Max: $73/mo 💡 3 tiers work best: Starter → Pro → Enterprise. The middle tier should be the obvious choice (anchor pricing). 📊 All Models Compared:
